notes: v1.14.40 - globe city-lights: blob -> fine point-cloud + texture crispness. Raw WebGL (NOT three.js; the directive's three.js APIs were translated to the actual hand-written GLSL/WebGL). (1) CITY DUST: the SPECTRUM seeding loop now emits SMALLER, DIMMER points so brightness builds from density (additive overlap of many tiny points) instead of a few big bright sprites - point size shrunk ~3x (0.5+lf*1.5 -> 0.24+lf*0.40), per-point brightness lowered (nbr 0.55+lf*0.80 -> 0.40+lf*0.55). Count unchanged (~18.9k, already dense + geography-seeded from the NIGHT_B64 night-lights mask). (2) CRISPNESS: earth textures (day/night/clouds, all power-of-two) get trilinear mipmaps (generateMipmap + LINEAR_MIPMAP_LINEAR) + max anisotropic filtering (EXT_texture_filter_anisotropic, guarded) applied after the async image upload - kills limb smear/shimmer at grazing angles. UNTOUCHED: packet arcs, CoreWeave hub beacons (already a separate dynamic tier), orbit rings, atmosphere/limb, earth-base brightness (already cool-dimmed x0.13-0.22, sun killed), wordmark, status, ENTER_ENABLED (still false), service worker. Skipped the directive sRGBEncoding (no three.js renderer; would shift the approved night palette). GATES: node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS; four stamps lockstep v1.14.40; cache bumped; CSS <style> braces unchanged 3468/3468 (JS-only); dct/sw CRLF + version.json LF. DEVICE-CHECK (John, iPhone, full close+reopen ONLINE to clear SW cache): lights read as a fine cloud (no magenta/violet blobs), metros glow by density, coastlines sharp at the limb (anisotropy), arcs + hub beacons intact and brighter than dust, orientation still correct (US not mirrored, London right limb). First-cut size/brightness - if metros too sparse, ADD points (bump the lf*lf*5 supersample) not size; if still blobby, shrink size further. Perf: ~18.9k points should hold 60fps; if it drops, cut count, never inflate size.
